intent: The MAC-policy audit set (catalog #25): /etc/selinux/ + /usr/share/selinux/ directory watches plus the MAC-label setxattr syscall rules. Directory watches are recursive (f3 2.2); the setxattr rules are auid-narrowed and rare (LOW).

rules.line: -a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S setxattr -S lsetxattr -S fsetxattr -F 'auid>=1000' -F 'auid!=unset' -k mac-policy

suspected_trap: /etc/selinux/ is a DIRECTORY watch -- recursive to the subtree bottom (f3 2.2), so it covers the whole policy tree, but on a stable host policy rarely changes (LOW-MEDIUM, not HIGH). The setxattr syscall rules are auid-narrowed and target MAC-label changes (rare, LOW). Mixed byte constants (900 watch / 1200 syscall). A naive impl might over-warn on the recursive dir watch or under-rank the setxattr group breadth.
